claims.text: In 2009 US Strategic Command produced CONPLAN 8888-11, 'Counter-Zombie Dominance Operations' — 31 pages of formal Pentagon planning for surviving a zombie apocalypse, declassified in 2011 via FOIA, with proper classification stamps, purpose statement, and rules of engagement.

with operational phases, threat
assessments, and rules of engagement.

statement.

undertake military operations to
preserve nonzombie humans from the
threats posed by a zombie horde. Because
zombies pose a threat to all non-zombie
human life, hereafter referred to as
humans, US Stratcom will be prepared to

document was declassified in 2011 thanks
to a foyer request. Now that's a quick
turnaround from classified the public. A
section of comp plan 8888 reveals why.
>> US stratcom con plan 8888 security
instructions. This document is

during times of crisis. Classified
capabilities used to counter zombies
will be addressed in appropriate orders
and crisis plan wi

claims.rationale: Verified. CONPLAN 8888-11 'Counter-Zombie Dominance' is a real document drafted by junior staff officers at US STRATCOM (Offutt AFB) in 2009-2010 for training purposes. It was unclassified for training value. The 31-page format and contents match what the host describes. The document went viral in 2014 when Foreign Policy published it. Very accurate setup.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 lays out a five-phase operational sequence (Shape, Deter, Seize Initiative, Dominate, Stabilize/Restore), with Phase 3 explicitly directing the US military to retreat to fortified positions and shelter in place for at least 40 days — leaving civilian populations on their own under whatever local law enforcement can sustain.

evidence.quote:

Stratcom watches for non-state actors
with chemical and biological WMDs. They
monitor disease vectors, outbreaks, and
epidemics. Phase one is deter. This
prevents enemies from creating zombies
and nuclear armed countries get notified
that counter zombie tactics aren't a
preparation for war. Phase two is seize
the initiative. Stratcom provides
security assistance to local
authorities. Quarantine zones get
created and enforced. Civilian areas
stay protected until they're overrun.
Then conplan 8888 enters phase three.
Dominate, respond, and counter. During
phase three, the US military locks down
and retreats to fortified positions for
at least 40 days.
>> US Stratcom Conplan 8888, phase three.
Upon receipt of a US Stratcom order, all
forces will begin preparations to
conduct combat operations against zombie
threats. US Stratcom will shelter all
personnel in place for at least 40 days.
>> After that, phases four and five focus
on stabilizing and restoring government.
Reconnaissance teams deploy to assess
the zombie threat.
>> US Stratcom con plan 8888 phase 4 and
five. No earlier than 40 days after the
initiation of phase 3 operations, US
Stratcom forces will initiate local
reconnaissance ops to determine the
severity of the remaining zombie threat.
Assess the physical and epidemiological
security of the local environment and
survey the status of basic services,
water, power, sewage, infrastructure,
and communications. If the threat is
neutralized, rebuilding begins. But as
long as zombies exist, the military
stays locked down. Conp

claims.rationale: Phase structure (Phases 0-5) and the 40-day shelter-in-place language are verifiable from the actual CONPLAN 8888 document. The 'civilians on their own' framing is correct: the document explicitly notes that the plan focuses on military command and control, not civilian protection during the lockdown phase. The plan does name local law enforcement as the front line until martial law is declared.

claims.text: The most likely zombie threat in CONPLAN 8888's analysis is pathogenic zombies — created by viruses, bacteria, or prions — and the host argues real-world precedents already exist: rabies, chronic wasting disease in deer, mad-cow-derived Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, and kuru from cannibalistic Papua New Guinea funeral rites all produce zombie-like symptoms.

evidence.quote:
pathogenic zombies created
when viruses or bacteria infect humans.
And the Pentagon isn't guessing.
Existing diseases could mutate and
trigger real outbreaks. Take rabies. The
virus attacks the central nervous
system, creating aggression and

mouth and attack anything that moves.
That foam carries the rabies virus. One
bite from a rabid animal means almost
certain death. Movies like 28 Days Later
and World War Z show virus-based
zombies. Animals also become zombies

misfolded protein that destroys brains

claims.rationale: Rabies, CWD, vCJD, and kuru are all real diseases with the host's described characteristics. Rabies does cause aggression and is fatal once symptomatic. CWD does cause neurological decline in deer/elk. vCJD (from BSE-infected beef) and kuru (from prion-contaminated brain tissue eaten in mortuary ritual by the Fore people of PNG) are documented prion diseases. Calling them 'zombie' is metaphor — these illnesses are debilitating and fatal but don't produce reanimation or contagion via bite in the Hollywood sense. The shape of the analogy is correct; the pathway from real disease to true zombie apocalypse is highly speculative.

claims.text: Real-world parasitic mind control already exists at scale: the jewel wasp (Ampulex compressa) injects venom that lets it lead a cockroach by the antenna into a burrow and lay eggs inside it; Ophiocordyceps fungus drives infected ants to climb and bite leaves before bursting from their heads; Toxoplasma gondii infects 30-50% of humans (often via cats) and is linked to risk-taking and personality changes.

jewel wasp attacks

claims.rationale: Ampulex compressa (jewel wasp) does indeed sting cockroaches in two precise nervous system locations and lead them by the antenna to a burrow — well-documented entomology. Ophiocordyceps unilateralis is a real fungus that manipulates ant behavior and produces fruiting bodies from the host's head. Toxoplasma gondii infection rates of 30-50% globally are accurate (US is closer to 11-15%; some countries 60%+). Behavior-altering effects in rodents are demonstrated. Effects on human behavior (slow reflexes, increased risk-taking, psychiatric correlations) are documented in correlational studies but causation remains contested. Mostly accurate biology with some inflated US prevalence.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 also recognizes Radiation Zombies (RZs) — created by nuclear accidents, dirty bombs, solar coronal mass ejections like the 1859 Carrington Event, or post-Chernobyl cellular damage — and Weaponized Zombies (WZs) deliberately engineered for military use via chemicals (e.g. BZ), CRISPR gene editing, or synthetic biology.

evidence.quote:
RZ radiation zombies
organisms become zombies after absorbing
extreme electromagnetic radiation.
Nuclear accidents, dirty bombs, cosmic
radiation. The Pentagon says any of
these can create zombies. Solar flares
bombard earth with charged particles. A
coronal mass ejection could expose
entire populations to dangerous
radiation. The 1859 caratin event
knocked out telegraph systems all over
the world. A similar event today could
deliver radiation doses that alter brain
chemistry on a massive scale. After
Chernobyl, emergency responders who
entered the reactor core experienced
rapid cellular breakdown. Some survivors
reported hallucinations and aggressive
behavior before dying.
But radiation doesn't always kill. We
covered how Chernobyl's dogs and wolves
adapted to high radiation. An entirely

resistant to cancer. If radiation can

a new type of human. And in the wrong
hands, that human becomes the perfect
soldier. Conplan 8888 calls these

claims.rationale: RZ and WZ are real categories in the CONPLAN 8888 document — that part is accurate. The 1859 Carrington Event is real (massive solar storm that disrupted telegraph systems). However the suggestion that a CME could produce 'zombies' or alter brain chemistry on a massive scale is the host's extrapolation; CMEs primarily threaten power grids and electronics, not biology. Chernobyl 'wolves resistant to cancer' is supported by recent research. BZ (3-quinuclidinyl benzilate) was indeed a US Army incapacitating agent in the 1960s. CRISPR-engineered zombies are pure speculation. Mixed: real categories from doc + real bioweapon precedents + speculative leap to actual zombie creation.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 includes Space Zombies (SZs) of extraterrestrial origin — citing the 21-day Apollo lunar quarantine and the 1908 Tunguska impact event as precedents — and concludes that nuclear weapons would be the most effective counter to space-borne contamination because incineration may be the only option once it cannot be contained.

claims.rationale: SZ is a real category in CONPLAN 8888. Apollo 11/12/14 astronauts did spend 21 days in quarantine in the Lunar Receiving Laboratory; the policy was discontinued from Apollo 15 onward. The 1908 Tunguska event flattened roughly 2,000 km² (~770 sq mi) of Siberian forest — close to but not 'over a thousand square miles.' Tardigrades surviving vacuum and radiation in low Earth orbit experiments is documented. The 'nuclear weapons as countermeasure' line is in the document. Most of this is accurate paraphrase of the (joke) document, with one minor area figure approximation.

claims.text: CONPLAN 8888 includes a separate Evil Magic Zombies (EMZs) category for occult-origin undead that may not be neutralized even by burning the corpse, and assigns the US Army Chaplain Corps as primary defense against them — implicitly conceding that defeating these threats may require divine intervention.

actually uses those words. The Pentagon
has an official position on supernatural
threats.

Oh man,
>> I know what it is. But yes, white

They've identified each type of zombie

>> US Stratcom con plan 8888 section

claims.rationale: EMZ is genuinely a category in CONPLAN 8888 and the Chaplain Corps is named — these are real document quotes. The document is a tongue-in-cheek planning exercise but the categories are accurately described. The Nazi Ahnenerbe (the host's 'Aderva' is a transcription mangling) was a real Himmler-led occult/research division. Churchill consulted Louis de Wohl, an astrologer. The US Army's psychic warfare research was the Stargate Project, also real. The framing of 'taking it seriously enough to assign the Chaplains' is a fair reading of the parodic document.

claims.text: The host argues that since The Walking Dead (Oct 2010), World War Z (filmed 2011, released 2013), and The Last of Us (2013) all premiered in the same window CONPLAN 8888 was finalized and declassified, the Pentagon may have used zombie-survival entertainment as a covert psychological-operations training program — getting millions of civilians to internalize prep, perimeter, and trust-no-one protocols 'with Netflix instead of boot camps.'

mentions annex C detailing space control
capabilities. Page after page references

public version. AnnexS covers special

document, footnotes point to information
marked secret or higher. The zombie
scenarios were unclassified. The real
military capabilities stayed hidden. The
most telling detail is buried in the
technical sections. Con plan 8888 works
alongside other military plans. Con plan
8035 for space operations. Conplan 8531
for flu

claims.rationale: The dates are correct: TWD premiered Oct 31, 2010; World War Z released 2013 (filming 2011-2012); The Last of Us shipped June 2013. CONPLAN 8888 was drafted 2009-2011. The host correctly debunks his own psyop hypothesis later in the verdict: zombie media long predated the document (Romero's Night of the Living Dead 1968, the Walking Dead comics 2003, etc.) and Hollywood pursued the trend for profit. The PSYOP interpretation is presented as conjecture and is essentially baseless.

claims.text: The unclassified CONPLAN 8888 document repeatedly references classified annexes — Annex C (space control), Annex S (special technical operations), Annex D (classified logistics) — that were stripped before public release, and works alongside operational plans CONPLAN 8035 (space), CONPLAN 8531 (pandemic), and CONPLAN 8099 (WMD), implying real underlying capabilities the zombie scaffolding hides.

evidence.quote:
classified annexes never released
publicly. The document repeatedly
mentions annex C detailing space control
capabilities. Page after page references
classified sections stripped from the
public version. AnnexS covers special
technical operations. AnnexD handles
classified logistics. Throughout the
document, footnotes point to information
marked secret or higher. The zombie
scenarios were unclassified. The real
military capabilities stayed hidden. The
most telling detail is buried in the
technical sections. Con plan 8888 works
alongside other military plans. Con plan
8035 for space operations. Conplan 8531
for flu

claims.rationale: Standard military operations plans do follow a fixed annex structure (A, B, C... S, etc., per CJCSM 3122 series), and a unclassified training document referencing those annex labels is consistent with normal Pentagon plan format — that's just how plans are templated, not necessarily evidence of secret zombie capabilities. CONPLAN 8035 (space), 8010 (strategic deterrence), 8044 are real STRATCOM plans. The host's inference that the zombie scaffolding 'hides' real capabilities is overreach; the structure is standard template inheritance. The factual existence of those annex categories and adjacent plans is correct.

claims.text: The host's practical survival advice (modeled on CONPLAN 8888's logic) is to evacuate cities within 48 hours of any outbreak, get to a remote farm/cabin/homestead with food and water, prefer quiet weapons over guns, prep a bug-out bag with LifeStraw/iodine/MREs/paracord/N95 masks/thermal blankets, and learn perimeter and signaling skills — because in collapse, the most dangerous threat is other people, not the undead.

evidence.quote:
When the outbreak starts, you have about

claims.rationale: Standard prepper-handbook advice. Evacuate-density / get-rural / quiet-weapons / prep-bag / 'humans are the real threat' is consistent with mainstream survival literature (FEMA, military SERE training, civilian preparedness guides). MREs nominal shelf life is 5-7 years not 30, and iodine pills are for thyroid blockade against radioactive iodine, not general 'radiation exposure.' Otherwise sound, conventional advice well-suited to a generic disaster — not specifically tied to the zombie scenario.

claims.text: In the verdict the host concedes CONPLAN 8888 is exactly what the Pentagon says it is — a junior-officer training exercise that uses an impossible enemy (evil magic zombies) so the planning practice doesn't create real diplomatic incidents — and that the entertainment-as-PSYOP timing is coincidence, since zombie media long predates 2009 (White Zombie 1932, Romero's Night of the Living Dead 1968, Dawn of the Dead 1978, Walking Dead comic 2003).

evidence.quote:

exercise. Junior officers created it to

impossible scenario avoids diplomatic

to defeat Russians or Chinese, the
enemies are evil magic zombies. Everyone
understands its practice. The zombie

the money. Remember we had 10 or 15

>> Yeah. Thanks a lot, Disney.

dismissing this as just a joke would be
a mistake. Con plan 888 connects to rea

claims.rationale: Correct historical chronology. White Zombie (1932) was the first feature zombie film. Night of the Living Dead (1968) established modern slow zombies. Dawn of the Dead (1978) cemented the genre. Robert Kirkman's The Walking Dead comic launched 2003 (not 2010). The 'practice on impossible enemy' justification matches what STRATCOM officers later said about why they used zombies (avoid diplomatic incidents that planning real-country attack scenarios would create). The host fairly debunks his own earlier PSYOP framing.

dependencies.rationale: The host explicitly retracts the entertainment-PSYOP hypothesis from c_0008 here in the verdict.

claims.text: Even though CONPLAN 8888 itself is a joke, the host argues its real value is the implicit Pentagon admission that there exist plausible scenarios — fast-spreading pandemics, infrastructure collapse, civic breakdown — where the US military explicitly cannot win and cannot protect civilians, who therefore have to function as their own first responders.

evidence.quote:
Conplan 8035, COP plan
8531, COP plan 8099. These aren't
exercises, they're operational.
[Music]
The plan's real value is seeing the
military acknowledge how fast society
could collapse. It admits there are
scenarios where the military can't win.
A plan to defeat zombies is hard for the
public to take seriously. But maybe
that's the point. Maybe Conplan 8888 is
a clever distraction. The story isn't in
the document they show us. It's in the
documents they hide. Conplan 8888
repeatedly references classified annexes
that were stripped from the public
version, but we know they exist. We just
don't know what they are. We know they
reference space-based operations, but we
don't k

claims.rationale: The takeaway — that CONPLAN 8888's value lies in modeling collapse and admitting limits — is the actual interpretation many commentators (including STRATCOM officers themselves) have offered. Pentagon planning for COVID-scale pandemic response, civic breakdown, etc. is real and ongoing. The 'civilians are first responders' framing is consistent with FEMA's longstanding 'Whole Community' approach. Reasonable concluding takeaway with mild conspiratorial flourish at the end about 'documents they hide.'
